chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. D – 175 of 2025 (Danish Ali Khokhar versus The State) Before: Mr. Mahmood A. Khan, J. Mr. Khalid Hussain Shahani, J. Date of hearing: 04.02.2026 Date of decision: 04.02.2026 Mr. Dhanraj, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Mansoor Ahmed Shaikh, Deputy Prosecutor General. O R D E R Mahmood A. Khan, J. – The applicant seeks bail in Crime No.89 of 2025, registered at Police Station Darya Khan Mari, District Naushahro Feroze, under Sections 9 Sr. No.3(c) of the Sindh Control of Narcotic Substances Act, 2024, wherein it is alleged that 1500 grams of charas have been recovered from the present applicant. 2. Learned Counsel for the applicant contends that description of alleged recovered charas has not been given in the FIR, whereas the chemical report obtained refers to only one piece. He further contends that the whole property, as such, has not been sent. As such, conviction may not be available in respect to the allegation made in the matter. Learned Counsel also contends that another FIR referred in the matter was the cause of roping the present applicant in the narcotics case, in which FIR police encounter has been alleged, wherein no firearm has been utilized. Learned Counsel relied upon the case of Suhino Khan v. The State (2009 YLR 1987). Cr. Bail App. No. D – 175 of 2025 Page 2 of 2 3. Learned DPG, however, severely opposes the bail contending that no mala fide has been established by the other side; that the samples for chemical analysis were sent within time, and the punishment covered by prohibitory clause concession of bail is not liable to be considered. 4. Having heard the learned Counsels and gone through the record, the grounds as raised by the applicant are found entertainable creating ground of further enquiry, and it is also observed that the compliance of Section 17 of the Sindh Control of Narcotic Substances Act, 2024 is not made available 5.

Learned DPG, however, severely opposes the bail contending that no mala fide has been established by the other side; that the samples for chemical analysis were sent within time, and the punishment covered by prohibitory clause concession of bail is not liable to be considered. 4. Having heard the learned Counsels and gone through the record, the grounds as raised by the applicant are found entertainable creating ground of further enquiry, and it is also observed that the compliance of Section 17 of the Sindh Control of Narcotic Substances Act, 2024 is not made available 5. Accordingly, bail application stands allowed, and the applicant is granted post-arrest bail against the surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and the P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
